title: Shortest and Lexicographically Smallest Beautiful String
titleCn: 最短且字典序最小的美丽子字符串

testcases.input: s = "100011001", k = 3 Output: "11001" Explanation: There are 7 beautiful substrings in this example: 1. The substring "100011001". 2. The substring "100011001". 3. The substring "100011001". 4. The substring "100011001". 5. The substring "100011001". 6. The substring "100011001". 7. The substring "100011001". The length of the shortest beautiful substring is 5. The lexicographically smallest beautiful substring with length 5 is the substring "11001".
testcases.expectedOutput: "11001" Explanation: There are 7 beautiful substrings in this example: 1. The substring "100011001". 2. The substring "100011001". 3. The substring "100011001". 4. The substring "100011001". 5. The substring "100011001". 6. The substring "100011001". 7. The substring "100011001". The length of the shortest beautiful substring is 5. The lexicographically smallest beautiful substring with length 5 is the substring "11001".

testcases.input: s = "1011", k = 2 Output: "11" Explanation: There are 3 beautiful substrings in this example: 1. The substring "1011". 2. The substring "1011". 3. The substring "1011". The length of the shortest beautiful substring is 2. The lexicographically smallest beautiful substring with length 2 is the substring "11".
testcases.expectedOutput: "11" Explanation: There are 3 beautiful substrings in this example: 1. The substring "1011". 2. The substring "1011". 3. The substring "1011". The length of the shortest beautiful substring is 2. The lexicographically smallest beautiful substring with length 2 is the substring "11".

testcases.input: s = "000", k = 1 Output: "" Explanation: There are no beautiful substrings in this example.
testcases.expectedOutput: "" Explanation: There are no beautiful substrings in this example.
